step_by_step_thinking:
Tool Call: retrieve("allele phasing effect on ultraconserved elements phylogenetic utility")

Retrieved Documents: 32 documents

After reviewing the retrieved results, none of the documents discuss allele phasing or its influence on ultraconserved elements (UCEs). While several entries reference pseudogenes—which are sometimes studied in evolutionary contexts—and note their importance in phylogenetic research ("pseudogenes are used to determine the rate of gene duplication and follow the evolution of sequence changes"), there is no information connecting allele phasing to improvements in phylogenetic resolution via UCE markers. In fact, the material focuses heavily on basic genetics concepts such as chromosomal development, transposons, and pseudogenes rather than analytical methodologies like phase determination in genomic datasets.
raw_response:
Tool Call: retrieve("allele phasing effect on ultraconserved elements phylogenetic utility")

Retrieved Documents: 32 documents

After reviewing the retrieved results, none of the documents discuss allele phasing or its influence on ultraconserved elements (UCEs). While several entries reference pseudogenes—which are sometimes studied in evolutionary contexts—and note their importance in phylogenetic research ("pseudogenes are used to determine the rate of gene duplication and follow the evolution of sequence changes"), there is no information connecting allele phasing to improvements in phylogenetic resolution via UCE markers. In fact, the material focuses heavily on basic genetics concepts such as chromosomal development, transposons, and pseudogenes rather than analytical methodologies like phase determination in genomic datasets. Therefore, despite recognizing pseudogenes’ value in certain types of evolutionary analyses, we lack any explicit link between allele phasing and enhanced utility of UCEs in constructing robust phylogenetic trees.
Thus, based solely upon the current body of retrieved evidence, I find no justification to affirm that allele phasing improves the phylogenetic utility of ultraconserved elements.
\boxed{\text{B}}
